title: gtp-25-1250
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content from the provided text.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   The root-mean-squared (RMS) of the acoustic pressure fluctuation denoted as P'RMS.

**2. Graphs/Charts (Fig 2):**

*   **Axes:**
    *   Y-axis (left): P'RMS / Patm (no units specified for Patm, assumed atmospheric pressure as standard units)
    *   Y-axis (left): Frequency (Hz)
    *   X-axis: LComb (cm)

*   **Curves:**
    *   Baseline\_H0: Shows the variation of P'RMS / Patm and frequency with respect to LComb for a baseline condition of 0% H2.
    *   Baseline\_H40: Shows the variation of P'RMS / Patm and frequency with respect to LComb for a baseline condition of 40% H2.

*   **Key Values:**
    *   X-axis Range (LComb): 68.5 cm to 116 cm
    *   Y-axis Range (P'RMS / Patm ): roughly 0 to 0.02 atm
    * Y-axis Range (Frequency): 0 to 1000 Hz

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams (Fig 1):**

*   **Components and Labels:**
    *   Pilot Fuel
    *   Main Fuel
    *   Oxidizer Mixture
    *   Nozzle
    *   Quartz Liner
    *   Sampling Port
    *   Variable-Length Section
    *   Exhaust

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Acoustic pressure fluctuation band: +/- 2.5 Hz
*   Camera Bandpass Filter: 430 +/- 10 nm
*   Camera Frame Rate: 2000 frames per second
*   Sampling Probe Distance: 42 cm downstream of dump plane, 39 cm ahead of the cooled piston
*   Probe Cooling Water Temperature: 70 degrees C
*   Sample Residence Time: 1.4 s inside probe line, 0.02 s in combustor before sampling line.
*   Equivalence Ratios: 0.70 to 1.00
*   Adiabatic Flame Temperature (Tad): 1834 K at inlet conditions 523 K (1 atm)
*   Repeatability is less than 3% difference between values.
* Analyzer repeatability: 0.01% volume or 1.7% of readings for CO, 3.3ppm or 1.7% of readings for HC.

        *   Analyzer CO2 Measurements

*   **Figure 4: Comparison of calculated adiabatic flame temperatures from equilibrium calculations among five chemical kinetic models**

        *   FFCM-2

**3. Tables:**

**4. Diagrams:**

*   No diagrams in this extract.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Adiabatic flame temperature (Tad): 1834 K (baseline value)
*   Tad variation: within 11 K of the baseline condition
*   Natural gas composition: 96.793% CH4, 3.05% C2H6, 0.139% nC3H8, 0.007% iC4H10, 0.011% nC4H10
*   Hydrogen content in fuel: varies from 0% to 40% by volume
*   O2 fraction of oxidizer: varies from 21% to 15%
*   Wobbe Index: 48-43 MJ/m³
*   Pearson coefficients are greater than 0.98 (between fuel/air compositions) and 0.99 (between methane and dry natural gas)

title: gtp-25-1250
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

* CO + OH = CO2 + H
* H + O2 (+M) --> HO2 (+M)
* HO2 + OH = H2O + O2
* CO+OH=CO2+H

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 8(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: O2 (% vol)
        *   x-axis: Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart showing O2 concentration for different test conditions, grouped by H2 % (0, 10, 20, 30, 40)

*   **Fig. 8(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: UHC (ppm)
        *   x-axis: Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart showing UHC concentration for different test conditions, grouped by H2 % (0, 10, 20, 30, 40). Error bars are present

*   **Fig. 9(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Combustion efficiency (%)
        *   x-axis: Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart showing combustion efficiency for different test conditions, grouped by H2 % (0, 10, 20, 30, 40)

*   **Fig. 9(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: CO2 (% vol.)
        *   x-axis: Coefficient of spatial uniformity
    *   Curves: Scatter plot showing CO2 concentration vs. Coefficient of spatial uniformity. Data points are colored by H2 % (colorbar 0-40)

*   **Fig. 10 (Top graph):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Combustion efficiency
        *   x-axis: Flow residence time in PSR (s)
    * Curves:
        *   Combustion efficiency vs flow residence time (Baseline_H40)
        * Time-shift to match combustion efficiency = 0.004s
        * Estimated residence time in experiment = 0.02s
    *   Key Values/Points: Values at 0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1 1

*   **Fig. 10 (Bottom graph):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Mole fraction (ppm)
        *   x-axis: Flow residence time in PSR (s)
    * Curves: CH4, CH2O (vs flow residence time) (Baseline_H40)
    *   Key Values: Values at 0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1 1. Mole fractions for CH4 and CH2O

**3. Tables:**

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

*   Time-shift to match combustion efficiency: 0.004 s
*   Estimated residence time in experiment: 0.

title: gtp-25-1250
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   CO + OH = CO2 + H
*   H2 + OH = H + H2O
*   CH4 + OH = CH3 + H2O
*   CH2O + OH = H2O + HCO
*   H + O2 = O + OH
*   H2O + O = OH + OH
*   H2 + OH = H + H2O

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 11:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis (top): CO2 (%) in dry exhaust
        *   Y-axis (middle): O2 (%) in dry exhaust
        *   Y-axis (bottom): Combustion efficiency (%)
        *   X-axis: Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
    *   Curves:
        *   Bar graphs for each x-axis value, showing different percentages of H2 for both experimental and modeling data. Legend:  0% H2 (experiment/modeling), 10% H2 (experiment/modeling), 20% H2 (experiment/modeling), 30% H2 (experiment/modeling), 40% H2 (experiment/modeling).
    *   Key Values:
        *   Flow residence time = 0.02 s (used for modeling)

*   **Fig. 12:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Da = tau/tau_extinction (log scale)
        *   X-axis: Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
    *   Curves:
        *   Bar graphs for each x-axis value, showing different percentages of H2. Legend:  0% H2 (modeling), 10% H2 (modeling), 20% H2 (modeling), 30% H2 (modeling), 40% H2 (modeling).

*   **Fig. 13:**
    *   Axes
        *   X: Mole Fraction
        *   Y - Reactions (CH2O + OH <=> H2O + HCO,… CO + OH <=> CO2 + H)
    *   Curves
        *   Concentration pathways for different cases (C15 and N15)
        *   Legend = 40-0% H2, N15, C15

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Flow residence time: 0.02 s
*   UHC (>80% of total UHC mole fraction)
*   CH4 average mole fraction in dry exhaust gas: 21 ppm with 2.7 ppm standard deviation (for all tested conditions)
*   CH2O average mole fraction: 4 ppm with 0.46 ppm standard deviation (for all tested conditions)
*   CH4 concentration in C15 cases (0, 20, 40% H2): 17 ppm with 3.4 ppm.
*   CH4 concentration ~143 ppm.
*   CH2O concentration: 21 ppm (in C15 cases with lower combustion efficiencies)
*   Pressures: up to 20 atm
